us_h1b.switch_conditions: New employer must file H-1B transfer petition before employment begins; portability applies after 180 days with an approved I-140
us_h1b.partner_work_notes: Partner eligible for H-4 EAD only after principal holder's I-140 is approved (typically 2–4 years after H-1B start)

us_opt.visa_name: F-1 Optional Practical Training (OPT / STEM OPT)
us_opt.switch_conditions: Work authorization flows from F-1 status, not an employer, so you may change employers freely on standard 12-month OPT. The 24-month STEM extension requires the employer to be enrolled in E-Verify and to file a Form I-983 training plan; report any employer change to your DSO within 10 days.
us_opt.partner_work_notes: F-2 dependents are not authorized to work in the US.

uk_skilled_worker.switch_conditions: Can switch employers but new employer must be a licensed sponsor; notify Home Office
uk_skilled_worker.partner_work_notes: Dependant visa grants full work rights with no restrictions

uk_graduate.switch_conditions: No sponsorship required; may work for almost any employer in any job, be self-employed, or change jobs freely. Non-extendable; switch to a sponsored route (e.g. Skilled Worker) before it expires.
uk_graduate.partner_work_notes: Partner/children already in the UK as your dependants can stay and work; new dependants generally cannot be added on this route.

ca_express_entry.switch_conditions: No employer tie — open work permit once PR is granted
ca_express_entry.partner_work_notes: Partner receives open work permit alongside principal applicant

ca_pgwp.switch_conditions: Open work permit — work for almost any employer in Canada and change jobs freely. Length matches the study program; master's programs of at least 8 months may receive up to a 3-year permit. Non-renewable. Since Nov 1, 2024 some programs require a qualifying field of study, plus a language-test result. Skilled work on a PGWP builds the experience that feeds Express Entry / Canadian Experience Class toward PR.
ca_pgwp.partner_work_notes: Under rules effective Jan 21, 2025 a spouse/common-law partner can get an open work permit only if the PGWP holder works in a TEER 0 or 1 occupation (or select TEER 2/3 roles on the eligible list).

au_tss_482.switch_conditions: Can switch to another approved sponsor; must notify Department of Home Affairs
au_tss_482.partner_work_notes: Secondary applicant on the same visa gets full work rights

au_485.visa_name: Temporary Graduate visa — Subclass 485 (Post-Higher Education Work stream)
au_485.switch_conditions: No employer tie — full work rights with any employer. Stay length depends on qualification: 2 years (bachelor or master by coursework), 3 years (master by research), 4 years (PhD). Generally must be under 35 at time of application. Bridges to skilled routes (subclass 189/190/482).
au_485.partner_work_notes: Family members included on the visa hold the same subclass 485 with full work rights.

de_eu_blue_card.switch_conditions: Within the first 12 months an employer change must be notified to the immigration authority (which may object within ~30 days); afterwards no approval is needed
de_eu_blue_card.partner_work_notes: Spouse receives full labour-market access with no separate work permit

de_skilled_worker.switch_conditions: Can switch employers without re-applying for a new visa; notify immigration authority
de_skilled_worker.partner_work_notes: Family reunion visa grants partner full work authorization from day one

de_opportunity_card.switch_conditions: Points-based job-seeker residence permit valid up to 12 months. No employer sponsor; while searching you may work part-time up to 20 hours/week and take two-week trial jobs with prospective employers. Requires proof of funds (~EUR 1,091/month as of 2025) plus a recognised qualification or 6 points, and basic German (A1) or English (B2). Once you secure a qualifying job you switch to a work permit such as the EU Blue Card or Skilled Worker visa.
de_opportunity_card.partner_work_notes: The Opportunity Card is a job-search permit; partner/family work rights generally apply only after you switch to a work residence permit (e.g. EU Blue Card).

fr_talent_passport.switch_conditions: Can switch employers within the Passeport Talent category; update with prefecture
fr_talent_passport.partner_work_notes: Passeport Talent famille card gives partner full and unrestricted work rights

fr_aps.visa_name: Residence permit 'Recherche d'emploi / création d'entreprise' (post-study, formerly APS)
fr_aps.switch_conditions: Valid 12 months and non-renewable; allows unrestricted work to find a first job or start a business linked to your studies. Requires a French master's (or equivalent) and a valid student permit when applying. Once employed in a related role you switch to a work or Passeport Talent permit.
fr_aps.partner_work_notes: Temporary post-study job-search permit — no dependent/partner work route until you switch to a longer-term work permit.
